items.title: Australian arrested after teenage girl's body found in a suitcase in Thailand

children.content: An Australian man is in custody after the body of a teenage girl was found in a suitcase in Thailand.

image.altText: Australian man arrested in Thailand.

thumbnail.altText: A man sits in a singlet behind a desk looking at the camera

items.title: These billionaires' kids are learning something most Australians are not

children.content: Rich and powerful families have a strong interest in one particular language, with experts saying it is no surprise given the country's rise.

image.altText: Mark Zuckerberg, Elon Musk, Donald Trump and Jeff Bezos are in front of a chalkboard with Chinese writing on it

items.title: Farewell: 27 things Virginia Trioli learned at the ABC

children.content: After almost 40 years in Australian journalism, 27 of them at the ABC, I am now in possession of enough mistakes to claim a small amount of experience.

image.altText: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Daughter of stabbing victims calls for better mobile coverage

children.content: The daughter of an elderly couple attacked in an alleged home invasion in northern NSW wants to see mobile coverage improved across regional areas.

image.altText: A woman stands in front of a shed

items.title: Volunteers say hotline told them to collect dead bird against government advice

children.content: South Australian citizen scientists say they called the Emergency Animal Disease Hotline to report a dead sea bird and were given advice that contradicts official government messaging.

image.altText: Johanna Williams photographs dead pied cormorant in shallow beach water

items.title: Australia's next opponent locked in after World Cup chaos

children.content: The Socceroos will meet Egypt in the knockout stage of the World Cup after a stunning finish in Group G. Follow live.

articles.title: Egypt and Iran push back against 'Pride Match' as rainbow flags wave

children.content: Rainbow flags wave around Seattle after Egypt and Iran asked FIFA to keep a Pride celebration out of its World Cup match.

image.altText: A woman ina pride flag stands outside a stadium.

articles.title: Live table: Which third-placed teams will make it to the knockouts?

children.content: The World Cup group stage is winding down, with third-placed teams anxiously watching other results to learn their fate. See the live table to see who is on track to progress.

image.altText: The Socceroos huddle in a circle

articles.title: Departure of Leckie and Italiano leaves Socceroos 'two soldiers down'

children.content: Australia enters the World Cup knockout rounds with just 24 players in the squad after veteran Mathew Leckie and right-back Jacob Italiano departed the camp with injuries.

image.altText: A soccer player is treated for an injury

items.title: Crows star confronts Power fan after magnificent Showdown goal

children.content: Josh Rachele has the last laugh following an exchange with a spectator at Adelaide Oval, while the Magpies enjoy their win over the Tigers, and the Blues make it six straight victories. Follow live.

image.altText: Josh Rachele confronts a spectator during Showdown 60.

items.title: Left out Blue makes Origin statement as Sea Eagles dominate Storm

children.content: Manly enforcer Haumole Olakau'atu, who was left out of the NSW team in State of Origin Game II, has made a statement to Blues selectors with a dominating first half against the Melbourne Storm. Follow live.

image.altText: Haumole Olakau'atu of the Sea Eagles is tackled by three defenders

items.title: In Beirut, a sacred ceremony also becomes display of Hezbollah's force

children.content: Tens of thousands of Shiite Muslims gathered in Beirut for the largest Ashura procession in years, as Hezbollah used the holy day to project strength despite growing pressure to disarm after months of war with Israel.

gallery.altText: Dozens of men, sitting cross-legged, all of them wearing black. Some are wearing white turbans.

thumbnail.altText: Dozens of men, sitting cross-legged, all of them wearing black. Some are wearing white turbans.

items.title: US strikes Iran after Trump calls Hormuz attack 'foolish' ceasefire breach

children.content: Iran's Revolutionary Guards vows a "swift and decisive" response, according to Iranian state television, claiming they repelled a US attack on Sirik Island, on the shores of the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands at a podium with US flags in the background.

items.title: Why hasn't the global economy completely collapsed?

children.content: An unfortunate side effect of the incompetent and erratic negotiating tactics of the Trump administration is that some of the underlying stories in the global economy can get overlooked.

image.altText: Donald Trump smiles while looking to the side.

items.title: Heartbreaking scenes emerging from earthquakes in Venezuela

children.content: After two powerful earthquakes, rescue teams from around the globe are in a race against time to pull survivors from the rubble.

articles.title: Why Venezuela doesn't have an 'earthquake preparedness culture'

children.content: As a rare one-two punch of earthquakes hit Venezuela this week, residents in the capital Caracas watched in horror as buildings shook and were sent "pancaking" before their eyes.

image.altText: A man involved in rescue efforts looks on from a damaged building in Venezuela.

gallery.altText: men carry an older man out of rubble
people stand on top of a collapsed building
people sit on rubble of a destroyed building

thumbnail.altText: men carry an older man out of rubble
people stand on top of a collapsed building
people sit on rubble of a destroyed building

items.title: Vixens and Fever meet for place in Super Netball grand final

children.content: Reigning champions Melbourne Vixens host Super Netball's preliminary final against the West Coast Fever, the team they defeated in last year's decider. Follow live.

image.altText: Kadie-Ann Dehaney of the Fever and Sophie Garbin of the Vixens bumping each other, competing for the ball

items.title: Rescuers pin hopes on satellite tracker to help save entangled humpback whale

children.content: An entangled humpback whale near Sydney is one of eight currently caught in marine debris off the New South Wales coast.

image.altText: Entangled humpback whale spotted off the NSW coast near Bondi

items.title: A warm and comforting gnocchi bake

children.content: "This would be something I'd love to make on a Sunday and have it bubbling away while you're doing odd jobs around the house."

gallery.altText: A pasta bake topped with melted gooey cheese is spooned out of a casserole dish.

thumbnail.altText: A pasta bake topped with melted gooey cheese is spooned out of a casserole dish.

items.title: At 80, John Williamson is ready to hang up his guitar

children.content: Now that folk and country music legend John Williamson is 80, he wants to spend less time touring and more time at home eating his own tomatoes.

image.altText: Man leaning against wooden fence with a guitar

items.title: Photographer captures elders' stories and WA's stunning southern landscapes

children.content: A photography exhibition in Albany shares the stories of Menang elders, who often have been overlooked in history.

image.altText: A woman wearing red clothes holds a framed photo.

items.title: The dry heart of Australia is now awash with water

children.content: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

gallery.altText: birds eye view of a bright pink lake, with a large body of blue water in the background
A channel of water weaves through greenery on either side

thumbnail.altText: birds eye view of a bright pink lake, with a large body of blue water in the background
A channel of water weaves through greenery on either side

items.title: Crime scene declared after armed man shot by police south of Brisbane

children.content: An armed man has been shot by police south of Brisbane after he allegedly confronted officers while carrying a rifle.

image.altText: Police tape in focus in front of a cream and blue house.

items.title: While teaching about pancreatic cancer, John realised he ticked every box

children.content: Clinical worker John McNeur didn't realise he was unwell until he was delivering a health workshop on pancreatic cancer in the remote Northern Territory — and he began ticking all of the boxes. Now he's on a mission to raise awareness.

image.altText: A close-up portrait of a bald man looking straight at the camera

items.title: Shock results from peptide test reveal 'life-threatening' ingredients

children.content: Doctors warn of an "enormous" risk of overdose after testing of a popular unapproved weight loss peptide reveals the product had double the strength of what was listed on its label.

image.altText: A vial of liquid sitting on a table with a label saying 'retatrutide 10mg'

items.title: Described as a 'talented athlete', Hussein Dehaini was gunned down at 33

children.content: Those who knew Hussein Dehaini say he could have been anything he wanted to be. But his life ended at 33, when he was gunned down in the middle of a Melbourne street.

image.altText: Hussein Dehaini pictured in a gym wearing shorts and no top

items.title: World Cup quick hits: Bedlam in Group H, Dua Lipa surprise and superstars warm the bench

children.content: Group H delivers us incredible drama, some of the World Cup's biggest stars find themselves warming the pine, and Dua Lipa makes a surprise appearance. Here are today's quick hits.

articles.title: 'Entitled to dream': Cabo Verde's World Cup run inspires the nation

children.content: Cabo Verde has secured the most unlikely spot in the World Cup knockouts, and the nation of 500,000 people is daring to dream.

image.altText: A coach is lifted into the air in celebration.

articles.title: Dembélé suspends time as France's favouritism heightens

children.content: Ousmane Dembélé showed patience and a command of time and space, to help propel France to the top of its group, and remain on course for World Cup glory.

image.altText: France's Ousmane Dembele has arms outstretched in celebration after scoring

gallery.altText: Dua Lipa, Roberto Lopes and Erling Haaland.

thumbnail.altText: Dua Lipa, Roberto Lopes and Erling Haaland.

items.title: 'Systemic issue': Abuse sees Canberra referees quitting after a year

children.content: Canberra soccer match officials say referee abuse is a "systemic issue" across all sporting codes, with increasingly hostile behaviour from spectators, players and coaches causing 44 per cent of Capital Football's referees to leave their roles after a year.

image.altText: A man in a cap stands on a soccer field talking to three youth referees.

items.title: 'God's gift to Indian cricket': The great expectations on teen wonder

children.content: On tour with India and on the brink of his international debut, the pressure is being heaped on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

image.altText: A batter holds his bat and helmet aloft after scoring a century.

items.title: Small plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building

children.content: Two bystanders have said an aircraft about the size of a car has crashed into Beijing's CITIC Tower.

image.altText: The CITIC tower with a hole in one of the windows.

items.title: Thousands in south-east Qld without power after attempted copper theft

children.content: Energex says it appears someone broke into a substation's cable pit and attempted to cut through an 11,000-volt cable, leaving about 13,000 south-east Queensland customers without power overnight.

image.altText: Kev Lavender substation

items.title: Singles saddle up and ditch the apps in search of old-school romance

children.content: Forget swiping right, singles are being urged to head to the bush in their search for real-life romance.

gallery.altText: Two men standing at a bar in a pub smiling and turning back to the camera

thumbnail.altText: Two men standing at a bar in a pub smiling and turning back to the camera

items.title: Sect leader convicted after girls found in trailer on US highway

children.content: A polygamous sect leader already serving a 50-year prison sentence in the US has been convicted of child abuse after girls were found in an unventilated trailer he was hauling through Arizona.

image.altText: A mug shot of Samuel Bateman wearing a navy prison-issued uniform.

items.title: More rain on the way for WA after storm damages roofs, downs trees

children.content: Perth residents are bracing for more wet weather after a powerful cold front damaged roofs and downed trees on Friday night.

image.altText: A tree lies across the roof of a house with SES crews in attendance

items.title: Hikers lost near Kosciuszko found by artificial intelligence drone

children.content: Rescuers were able to locate the hikers less than five hours after they were reported missing.

gallery.altText: A grey image with a red dot.

thumbnail.altText: A grey image with a red dot.

items.title: One Nation won Farrer. Winning over Farrer is another matter

children.content: Farrer residents decisively voted to change their once-safe seat. They are now putting Pauline Hanson and One Nation to the test.

image.altText: man and woman cheer in front of an orange backdrop.

items.title: Defence home loan subsidy to change after veterans left worse off

children.content: Critics say the scheme, which offers subsidised home loans, lumps Defence members and veterans with uncompetitive rates.

image.altText: Medals pinned to a soldier's uniform.

items.title: Should dementia patients be able to choose to die?

children.content: Families and dementia patients say they should be able to die on their own terms, so why isn't dementia treated like other incurable diseases? What would it take to change Voluntary Assisted Dying laws?

gallery.altText: An elderly man holds the hand of his adult son, walking in a garden.

thumbnail.altText: An elderly man holds the hand of his adult son, walking in a garden.
